description: The Sc.B. program in Mechanical Engineering (ME) has a long tradition of excellence at Brown. ME is one of the most versatile engineering disciplines as it explores a wide range of systems spanning fluids and solids. Students from this concentration learn the science of motion, designing and analyzing systems that shape our world:  From high-precision nanomechanical machines to large-scale aerospace systems. At Brown, ME students carve unique paths leveraging computational, analytical, or experimental methodologies with other areas of interest in the spirit of the open curriculum. They become distinguished scholars, engineering innovators, and management leaders. The curriculum in ME is intended to provide students with a broad interdisciplinary foundation complemented by specialized training in mechanical systems and fluid/thermal systems.

tracks.name: Standard Program for the Sc.B. degree

groups.name: 1. Core Courses:

courses.name: Applied Ordinary Differential Equations with Theory
Applied Partial Differential Equations I with Theory
Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming and Computer Science

groups.name: 2. Upper-Level Mechanical Engineering Curriculum: Complete at least 6 courses from the following groups:
Mechanical Systems: At least one course from:
Fluids/Thermal Systems: At least one course from:

courses.name: Fluid Mechanics of Aerospace and Energy Systems
Principles of Heat and Mass Transfer

groups.name: Capstone: At least one course from the following must be taken in the final two semesters:
Design Electives: Up to two courses from:
Bioengineering Electives: Up to two courses from:
Robotic and Control Systems Electives: Up to two courses from:
Engineering Analysis and Computation Electives: Up to two courses from:

courses.name: Advanced Numerical Methods for Data, Simulation, and Optimization

groups.name: Energy and Environmental Engineering Electives: Up to two courses from:
Interdisciplinary Electives: Up to one course from:

courses.name: Analysis and Design of Electronic Circuits
Water Supply and Treatment Systems: Technology and Sustainability

groups.name: 3. Upper Level, Advanced Science Course: At least one course from:
4. General Education Requirement: At least four approved courses must be taken in humanities and social sciences
